title: artemisia dracunculus subsp. glauca
intro: Artemisia dracunculus subsp. glauca, also known as wild tarragon, is a subspecies of the lesser known artemisia species, primarily found in Europe, North Africa, and parts of Asia. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and potentially lowering blood sugar levels. The leaves of the plant can be used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate digestive issues and promote overall well-being.
